title: qa-title-validation: reject newlines and null bytes in titles
body:
# Goal

Reject (or sanitize) titles that contain control characters
— specifically newlines and null bytes — at the CLI/storage
boundary so downstream surfaces don't corrupt or silently
lose data.

End state: `jjf new -t $'foo\nbar' ...` returns a typed
error; `jjf new -t $'a\x00b' ...` returns a typed error.
`jjf ls` and `jjf ready` text rows are guaranteed to be
single-line.

# Why now

QA red-team round 2026-06-23 (see "qa-redteam" handoff)
found two title-validation gaps:

1. **Embedded null byte silently truncated.**
   `jjf new -t $'a\x00b' --slug t` produces a ticket
   with `title = "a"`. Data loss before storage. Happens
   somewhere between argv parsing and JSON serialization.

2. **Embedded newline accepted, but corrupts `ls` / `ready`
   text output.** The tab-separated row format
   `<id>\t<status>\t<labels>L\t<title>` has no escaping
   rule, so a newline-bearing title splits across multiple
   text rows. Downstream `awk` / `jq` / shell pipelines
   break.

Both are easy to hit with hand-written prose titles from
the asterinas migration. Better to surface the rejection
at create-time than to discover ticket loss / scripting
breakage during migration.

# Scope

## Storage / CLI

- Add `Error::InvalidTitle { reason: TitleReason }` where
  `TitleReason` is one of:
  - `Empty` (already covered; keep).
  - `Newline` (rejects `\n` and `\r` anywhere in the
    title).
  - `NullByte` (rejects `\0`).
  - `ControlChar { codepoint: u32 }` (rejects any
    `c.is_control()` other than the above; surfaces the
    specific codepoint for the operator).
- Apply validation in `IssueDraft::validate()` or
  equivalent — wherever `slug` and `body` are validated
  today.
- Same validation applies on `jjf update --title <text>`.

## Length cap (out of scope, file separate ticket if
desired)

Asterinas tree has titles up to ~200 chars and we have no
length cap today. The 500-char beads limit
(`reference/beads/internal/types/types.go`) is a reasonable
prior art. Not in this ticket.

## CLI error mapping

- `docs/cli-json.md` gets a new error kind
  `invalid_title` with `details.reason: "newline" |
  "null_byte" | "control_char" | "empty"` and (for
  control char) `details.codepoint: u32`.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf new -t $'a\nb'` exits 2 with `invalid_title`.
- [ ] `jjf new -t $'a\x00b'` exits 2 with `invalid_title`.
- [ ] `jjf new -t $'a\tb'` exits 2 or accepts (decide:
  tabs in titles break `ls` parsing too).
- [ ] `jjf update <id> --title $'a\nb'` exits 2 the same way.
- [ ] CLI integration test reproducing each rejection.
- [ ] Storage test that `IssueDraft::validate()` enforces
  the rule independently of the CLI.
- [ ] `docs/cli-json.md` documents the new error kind.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.

# Out of scope

- Sanitize-instead-of-reject (the alternative — replace
  `\n` with `\\n` in display only). Not chosen because
  the body field would still corrupt git-blame-like
  surfaces; rejection at the boundary is cleaner.
- Title length cap. File separately.
- Sanitizing on render (the alternative for newline).
  We're choosing reject-at-write because hand-written
  prose migration is the immediate use case.

# Pointers

- Current validation site: `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`
  `IssueDraft` (where `empty title` is rejected today).
- Slug validation as precedent: same file.
- Error kinds today: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` `Error`
  enum.
- CLI error envelope shape: `docs/cli-json.md`.
- Tab-separated `ls` row format documented in
  `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` `run_ls` (line ~1977).
- Repro: QA round 2026-06-23 tests 4, 5.
